title: What Evangelion Units mean Question
wikitext:
[]: What Evangelion Units mean? [Question]             Quote Post by Jere98 » Sun Apr 03, 2016 6:04 pm Hi, I´ve started watching Evangelion lately and while I was watching Episode 26 from the original Series [I mean, EoTV] I noticed (understood) that Shinji says that piloting the Eva-01 is the only thing that he had , and the recognition that he get from it makes him happy. But his identity could disappear or make others not recognise him becase of him as a person, insted of for being the pilot from the Evangelion Unit 01. My question would be, what does it mean piloting the Evangelion life? I mean, what Hideaki Anno tried to mean with piloting the Evangelion to the audience? Is something from daily life that normal people could associate and identify in their lives or its just for Shinji's case and don't have anything releated with his audience life? Also, I'd like to say that I watched it in Spanish latin-american dub, so maybe it could be a missunderstood with the translation, or maybe I understood wrong what Shinji said during the episode. Well, just that. Thank you all

[]: Re: What Evangelion Units mean? [Question]             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 03, 2016 10:50 pm In part, Eva is a warning against investing too much of your identity in one thing. If you only exist for one thing, and that one thing is taken away or lost, then, afterward, you have nothing and have to start over from the beginning. It's like being a tree with a very shallow root bed -- you can't hold strong against the adversity of the elements, and your ability to derive nourishment from your surroundings is very limited. A tree with deep, thick, widely spread roots, however? It has considerably more options for sustenance and can take much more punishment before it falls down. The human equivalent of the latter would be a person with multiple passions -- no one single reason for being -- and a sturdy emotional support network. At the time that he made NGE, Anno was having something of a crisis regarding his identity as an otaku. In Japan, otaku have a reputation for being socially reclusive individuals with an obsessive interest in maybe one or two things. Anno's identity is inextricably tied to otaku culture; despite the shame associated with it, he could not simply disown it and become something else. But, perhaps, he could be an otaku and other things. This exploration of identity is manifest with him also exploring live action film-making around the time of NGE's completion, perhaps as a measure against being boxed into animation for the rest of his creative career. There is also a considerable psychoanalytical dimension to NGE, wherein the Evangelions represent (and, spiritually, are ) "the mother" for both Shinji and Asuka. Their dependency on the Evas embodies the concept of "mother being the first other" -- that is, the first separate individual an infant would be exposed to (working from Freudian assumptions, of course), which it also cannot yet recognize as truly separate but views as an extension of itself. For the ego to undergo maturation, the child must become fully separate from its mother, become its own being. Piloting Evangelion reduces Shinji and Asuka to a symbolically fetal state, so they become even LESS than the baby at the breast unable to differentiate between Mother and Self; but, in losing the ability to pilot Evangelion, they also gain the ability to discover who they actually are. (This is all done from memory, so apologies if it's factually off-base here and there!)

[]: Re: What Evangelion Units mean? [Question]             Quote Post by zlink64 » Mon Apr 04, 2016 12:16 am I don't disagree with above but I think a lot of what you are asking about I think Anno might've got from here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qpXNRrtuo38 And https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xxrmOHJQRSs All of Shinji's talk of purpose and choice contemplation during the show and why he pilots eva and him deciding that his sense of self is subjective and can change during the conclusion is what makes me think those links I posted are relevant. I wouldn't worry to much about misunderstanding the show because based off the way you are questioning the show I think your response to it is close to what might've Anno wanted from a viewer. I mean that., IMO, you are probably "getting it" in the way Anno might've wanted.

[]: Re: What Evangelion Units mean? [Question]             Quote Post by Jere98 » Mon Apr 04, 2016 8:05 am Thank you both for you answears , I could understand them very well and now Episode 26 too. After I make this post I've started thinking why Shinji sees an other reality during the episode, now I get it too: it was to see that piloting the Eva is not the only thing that he could do with his life, he could be anything he wanted to be, because he is free (now I want to see the freedom scene again to understand that too). Thanks a lot again, I'm really enjoying the series , despite the complex it is for me to understand what somethings really mean

[]: Re: What Evangelion Units mean? [Question]             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Apr 05, 2016 2:01 pm Jere98 wrote: Thanks a lot again, I'm really enjoying the series , despite the complex it is for me to understand what somethings really mean
